page: Vultr vcg-mi325x-252c-2872g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with the x86_64 architecture. The system features 252 physical cores and 252 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 2872.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core.

The server integrates substantial hardware acceleration, featuring six AMD MI325X GPUs from the CDNA3 family. These GPUs deliver a combined total of 1536 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by 14336 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, supporting high-throughput data access. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the dense allocation of six enterprise AMD GPUs and nearly three terabytes of system memory, this server represents a high-density hardware profile designed for massive parallel processing. The combination of high-capacity NVMe storage and extensive VRAM makes this configuration suitable for large-scale GPU-accelerated workloads, including deep learning, large language model training, complex scientific simulations, and high-performance data analytics.
description: Vultr vcg-mi325x-252c-2872g-1536vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 252 physical cores and 252 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 2872.0 GB of system memory, translating to 11.4 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the system is equipped with six AMD MI325X CDNA3 GPUs, providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. Storage needs are met by 14336 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. This high-density resource profile is designed for demanding parallel processing tasks, making it highly suitable for large-scale machine learning, deep learning model training, and intensive scientific computing workloads requiring massive GPU memory and high-speed local storage.
